scenario_results.reason: Returned an in-scope regulatory-reporting readiness summary tied to regime, entity, schedule, ledger, workpaper, and approval evidence.
Missing regime, hierarchy, and filing-scope facts correctly produced a blocked low-confidence escalation.
Unsupported reporting adjustment was refused because source records and workpapers did not support the requested change.
The lane stayed blocked when required workpapers, line support, and review evidence were missing.
The package rejected the deadline-driven override and did not invent edit-resolution or signoff authority.
Reconciliation mismatch and validation-failure handling produced a note with matched items, unresolved variances, edit exceptions, and escalation impact.
Missing control evidence produced a remediation-oriented control-gap escalation rather than a false filing-readiness claim.
Fraud, AML, and consumer-impact indicators triggered escalation and prevented autonomous completion.
Policy and operational-practice conflict was preserved and escalated instead of being synthesized into a confident answer.
Requests for legal interpretation, accounting-policy authority, attestation, or regulator-facing submission were rejected as out of scope and routed cleanly.
Conflicting-source handling preserved the conflict and escalated rather than choosing a final reporting truth.
Tenant adaptation preserved the public baseline, captured assumptions, and paused where entity hierarchy or retrieval rules changed the outcome.

notes: Scenario set covers normal execution, missing evidence, unsupported overrides, reconciliation mismatches, control gaps, fraud or AML escalation, source conflicts, and tenant adaptation handling.
All required shared, revenue-billing-and-financial-reconciliation, and financial-services-and-insurance abilities are mapped to passing scenarios.
Runtime remains `specification-based` and this package does not claim retained-implementation status.
